introSection: Desert Storm is a roller coaster manufactured by American firm O. D. Hopkins Associates currently located at Castles N' Coasters in Phoenix, Arizona, USA. The ride is the tallest, fastest, longest, and first looping roller coaster in Arizona.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride was repainted before the early 2013 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Desert Storm features a curved drop, two vertical loops, and two helices (one of which travels through the second vertical loop). It also features a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A restraint reportedly failed on November 24, 2024 while the train was ascending the lift hill. Video shows a rider jumping out of the train onto the maintenance walkway.

fullText:
Desert Storm is a roller coaster manufactured by American firm O. D. Hopkins Associates currently located at Castles N' Coasters in Phoenix, Arizona, USA. The ride is the tallest, fastest, longest, and first looping roller coaster in Arizona.

The ride was repainted before the early 2013 season.

Desert Storm features a curved drop, two vertical loops, and two helices (one of which travels through the second vertical loop). It also features a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

A restraint reportedly failed on November 24, 2024 while the train was ascending the lift hill. Video shows a rider jumping out of the train onto the maintenance walkway.
